title: Recover prerelease publish after mypy-fix commit and failed v0.0.1rc0 tag runs
description:
Context:
The v0.0.1rc0 publish workflow has already failed twice on the tag. A later local commit appears to fix CI mypy failures, but the tag still points to the older commit.

Evidence:
- `git tag --list v0.0.1rc0` exists and points at a33e676.
- local HEAD is e56c188 and is ahead of origin/main by 1 commit (`fix: resolve mypy errors so publish workflow can gate cleanly`).
- GitHub run 26073648388 failed mypy on Python 3.11/3.12/3.13 with 10 type errors.
- PyPI trade-trace JSON endpoint returned 404 during review; package is not published.

Decision required:
Choose an immutable recovery path: either move/re-push the rc0 tag if acceptable for this pre-release validation, or bump to 0.0.1rc1 and tag a fresh prerelease after local gates pass. Do not rerun the old failed tag workflow unless it checks out the corrected commit.
acceptance_criteria:
- Release-critical local gates pass: `python3 -m ruff check .`, `python3 -m pytest -q`, and `mypy src` in a dev environment.
- pyproject.toml, src/trade_trace/version.py, and chosen git tag are consistent.
- A fresh publish workflow run reaches green through test/build/publish.
- PyPI package page exists and lists the chosen prerelease version.
- trade-trace-off close reason records the chosen tag recovery policy and run ID.

title: Align smoke test version contract with rc0 prerelease policy
description:
Context:
The package metadata and version module were bumped to 0.0.1rc0 for the prerelease, but the smoke test still hard-codes the final 0.0.1 version.

Evidence:
- pyproject.toml:7 has version = "0.0.1rc0".
- src/trade_trace/version.py defines __version__ = "0.0.1rc0".
- tests/test_smoke.py:9 asserts trade_trace.__version__ == "0.0.1".
- `python3 -m pytest tests/test_smoke.py -q` fails with AssertionError: '0.0.1rc0' != '0.0.1'.

Decision:
If this remains an rc publish, make the smoke test assert consistency with the package/version source of truth rather than hard-coding 0.0.1. If cutting final 0.0.1 instead, update pyproject.toml, version.py, and tag policy together.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Confirm `pyproject.toml` and `src/trade_trace/version.py` both say `0.0.1rc0`.
2. Run `python3 -m pytest -q tests/test_smoke.py`.
3. Observe `test_package_importable` fail because it expects `trade_trace.__version__ == "0.0.1"`.
acceptance_criteria:
- `python3 -m pytest -q tests/test_smoke.py` passes.
- pyproject.toml and src/trade_trace/version.py remain consistent.
- CI tag version check still verifies tag/version alignment.
- The test policy is explicit for prereleases versus final releases.

title: Fix exporter SECRET_PATTERNS compatibility alias for security tests
description:
Context:
The current full pytest run cannot collect tests because tests/security/test_redacted_exports.py imports SECRET_PATTERNS from trade_trace.exporter.

Evidence:
- tests/security/test_redacted_exports.py:24-28 imports SECRET_PATTERNS from trade_trace.exporter.
- tests/security/test_redacted_exports.py:71-80 asserts set(SECRET_PATTERNS) equals api_key/slack_token/ethereum_address/jwt.
- src/trade_trace/exporter.py:187-193 documents that SECRET_PATTERNS is kept as a backwards-compatible alias, but no symbol is defined/exported.
- src/trade_trace/security/patterns.py:51-56 defines BUILTIN_PATTERNS with the expected names.
- `python3 -m pytest -q` and `python3 -m pytest tests/security/test_redacted_exports.py -q` fail at collection with ImportError.

Suggested fix direction:
Expose a compatibility alias from exporter using the canonical security pattern registry, without changing scanner behavior.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. From a clean checkout, run `python3 -m pytest -q tests/security/test_redacted_exports.py`.
2. Observe collection fail with `ImportError: cannot import name 'SECRET_PATTERNS' from 'trade_trace.exporter'`.
3. Confirm `src/trade_trace/exporter.py` documents SECRET_PATTERNS as a compatibility alias while no symbol is exported.
acceptance_criteria:
- `from trade_trace.exporter import SECRET_PATTERNS` succeeds.
- `python3 -m pytest -q tests/security/test_redacted_exports.py` passes.
- Full pytest collection proceeds past test_redacted_exports.py.
- `python3 -m ruff check .` remains clean.

description:

Cover explicit metadata_json credential injection in no-credentials policy

Evidence:

title: Define signed quantity convention and fix projection realized P&L sign coverage
description:
Context:
Domain: reports-projections-export
Candidate: DEBT-022
Raw source: lane:5#candidate-2
Artifacts: audits/no-tech-debt-20260518/lane-reports/lane-5.md

Technical-debt claim:
Define signed quantity convention and fix projection realized P&L sign coverage

Evidence:
projections.py realized_pnl += (price - avg_entry_price) * qty_delta; long open +100 close -100 at profit yields negative P&L under tests’ convention.

## Steps to Reproduce
Seed a long-like position with open quantity_delta=100 at 0.40 and close quantity_delta=-100 at 0.50, then rebuild projections; formula `(0.50 - 0.40) * -100` produces negative realized P&L for a profitable close.

Carrying cost / risk:
Derived positions/report.pnl can persist inverted realized P&L.

Target paydown:
Define signed convention and add rebuild tests for profitable/losing long and short closes.

title: Add replay-safe idempotency to strategy.update
description:
Context:
Domain: domain-tools-ledger-memory-playbook
Candidate: DEBT-019
Raw source: lane:4#DTLMP-003
Artifacts: audits/no-tech-debt-20260518/lane-reports/lane-4.md

Technical-debt claim:
Add replay-safe idempotency to strategy.update

Evidence:
strategy.update accepts idempotency_key and emits strategy.updated, but unlike strategy.create does not call check_idempotency_replay before update.

## Steps to Reproduce

title: Make memory.reflect node + about-edge write truly atomic
description:
Context:
Domain: domain-tools-ledger-memory-playbook
Candidate: DEBT-017
Raw source: lane:4#DTLMP-001
Artifacts: audits/no-tech-debt-20260518/lane-reports/lane-4.md

Technical-debt claim:
Make memory.reflect node + about-edge write truly atomic

Evidence:
memory.reflect docstring/docs claim one atomic operation, but implementation calls _memory_retain in one UnitOfWork then inserts about edge in a second UnitOfWork.

## Steps to Reproduce
Create a memory.reflect call where node retention succeeds and about-edge insertion/event emission fails; current implementation uses two UnitOfWork transactions so the reflection node can commit without the about edge.

Carrying cost / risk:
Failure between transactions can leave orphan reflection nodes despite no-orphan invariant.

Target paydown:
Refactor reflect to insert node/event/edge/event in one transaction; add forced-failure rollback test.

- No broad rewrite; no unrelated behavior/product changes; preserve documented local/offline security boundaries unless this row explicitly revises docs.

title: Implement review.bundle with deterministic hash and source redaction
description:
Context:
review.bundle is registered as a contract stub, but docs define it as the bounded external-review package surface.

Evidence:
- README.md:49 says review.bundle is a contract stub with P1 implementation pending.
- docs/architecture/reports.md §5 defines review.bundle semantics.
- docs/architecture/security.md says review.bundle redaction is contract-only and P1 implementation.
- src/trade_trace/tools/review_bundle.py raises UNSUPPORTED_CAPABILITY while schema includes bundle_hash.

Scope:
Select bounded cases using ReportFilter, include requested related records, enforce source redaction/sensitive omission, and produce deterministic canonical JSON with stable bundle_hash.
acceptance_criteria:
- review.bundle returns deterministic JSON and stable bundle_hash for identical inputs.
- `sources.redaction_status='sensitive'` sources are omitted with caveat counts; `redacted` sources omit body/extracted_text/excerpt/summary/note while preserving metadata.
- max_records/include flags/filter behavior match docs.
- Tests cover hash stability, redaction/omission, bounds, filter/drilldown consistency, and no LLM/advice output.

title: Implement import.validate/import.commit JSONL replay path
description:
Context:
JSONL import is documented as the P1 replay path but current tools are contract stubs.

Evidence:
- README.md:48-49 says import.validate/import.commit are contract stubs and P1 implementation is pending.
- docs/architecture/imports.md:5-11 says P1 ships actual import command path.
- src/trade_trace/tools/imports.py handlers raise UNSUPPORTED_CAPABILITY.

Scope:
Implement validation and commit for JSONL files/directories emitted by the exporter, using core dispatch and respecting transport metadata stripping, id strategy, idempotency, and transaction mode.
acceptance_criteria:
- `import.validate` parses file and directory inputs, validates line shape/tool existence/schema/id strategy, reports errors without writes.
- `import.commit` replays valid lines through core dispatch with documented transaction modes and rollback behavior.
- Exporter-output replay ignores underscore transport metadata.
- Tests cover validate no-write, atomic rollback, per-row partial progress, idempotent replay, ID-strategy conflict, and forward-reference rejection.

title: stdio MCP server: wire mcp SDK into serve_stdio + entry point
description:

Critical files:
- src/trade_trace/mcp_server.py:40-66 — replace serve_stdio() stub.
- pyproject.toml — add console_script entry trade-trace-mcp = trade_trace.mcp_server:serve_stdio_main. The mcp>=1.0 optional dep is already declared.
- src/trade_trace/cli.py — optional: add 'tt mcp serve' subcommand wrapping serve_stdio().

Reuses:
- src/trade_trace/core.py::dispatch — transport-agnostic.
- src/trade_trace/contracts/tool_registry.py::ToolRegistry — walk by_name for list_tools().
- src/trade_trace/mcp_server.py::mcp_call — the stdio server is a thin wrapper around it.

Actor identity: server resolves actor_id from MCP_ACTOR_ID env var, defaulting to 'mcp:default' (matches the in-process shim's pattern).

Open question to resolve during impl: confirm the mcp SDK 1.x exposes mcp.server.Server vs FastMCP (look up the SDK docs before writing the bridge).

Actor_id resolution from MCP_ACTOR_ID env var documented. ≥3 named tests in tests/integration/test_mcp_stdio_server.py covering: initialize handshake, list_tools shape, round-trip call vs shim.

title: EPIC: Agent-ready — stdio MCP server, input schemas, agent guide, embeddings
description:
Post-MVP epic to make trade-trace consumable by external MCP-speaking LLM agents.

The trade-trace-1mx MVP epic closed with 834 tests but every path uses the in-process mcp_call shim. The actual stdio MCP server is a NotImplementedError stub (src/trade_trace/mcp_server.py:54-66). Without this, Claude Desktop / Claude Code / Cursor / etc. literally cannot connect.

This epic closes that gap end-to-end:
- stdio MCP server (mcp SDK bridge to ToolRegistry).
- Auto-derived per-tool inputSchema from example_minimal payloads.
- AGENT_GUIDE.md walkthrough of the journal loop.
- Client config docs for Claude Code, Cursor/Windsurf/Cline, Claude Desktop.
- CI smoke test (subprocess + tools/list + tools/call round-trip).
- Embeddings opt-in (sqlite-vec + bge-small + OS keyring + tt memory reindex) — pulled forward from previously-deferred trade-trace-a4p.
- Living dogfood-findings tracker bead.
- Test QC + Docs QC + Gate.

User decisions captured in plan-mode Q&A 2026-05-18 (plan file: /home/hermes/.claude/plans/composed-riding-whale.md):
- Support all 3 host families (Claude Code, IDE-generic, Claude Desktop).
- Auto-derive inputSchema from example_minimal (faster than per-tool Pydantic models; can upgrade later).
- Include the embeddings opt-in in this epic, dogfood-tracker bead, and CI smoke test.

Done definition: a user can pip install trade-trace[mcp], add the MCP server to a host config, restart the host, see ~40 tools via tools/list each with a valid inputSchema, and drive the full PRD §10 loop from natural-language prompts. Optionally enable semantic recall via tt config set embeddings.provider local.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Stdio MCP server is shippable through a real host config, not only the in-process shim.
- tools/list exposes every registered tool with valid inputSchema.
- Agent guide and client config docs are truthful against implementation.
- Embeddings opt-in paths are implemented and off-by-default safety is proven.
- Test QC, docs QC, and final gate beads close with direct command/file evidence.
notes: Planning review 2026-05-19: related living dogfood tracker trade-trace-4ng to this epic for navigation because final gate trade-trace-dhs requires dogfood tracker open + receiving notes.

title: Final Verification: Trade Trace MVP dogfood scenario and contract audit
description:
Prove the MVP learning loop is implemented and useful enough to close the root epic.

## Scope
In scope:
- Run the full deterministic test suite and lint/type checks.
- Run or create an end-to-end dogfood/smoke scenario aligned to PRD §10: journal.init, decisions/forecasts/outcomes, scoring, reports/coach, reflections, playbook update, later adherence, recall during later thesis.
- Verify zero trade execution paths, zero broker/wallet credentials, zero default outbound network, CLI/MCP parity, docs truthfulness, and P1/P2 deferral accuracy.

Out of scope:
- Shipping deferred P1/P2 features just to satisfy final verification.
- Any public/remote publication unless separately authorized.

## Known leads
- PRD §10 MVP definition of done
- README MVP vertical slice
- Subagent review recommended deterministic dogfood protocol and final contract audit.

## Validation
- python -m ruff check .
- python -m ruff format --check .
- python -m mypy src tests
- python -m pytest
- trade-trace journal init --home /tmp/trade-trace-smoke
- trade-trace journal status --home /tmp/trade-trace-smoke
- trade-trace report filter_schema --home /tmp/trade-trace-smoke

## Decision readiness
human-decision-readiness: afk_capable
resolved decisions:
- Product boundary from README/VISION/PRD: local AI-only journal/memory/calibration substrate; no trade execution; no broker/wallet credentials; no external trading-data fetching.
- MVP scope is PRD M0-M4 only; P1/P2 implementations remain out of scope except contract stubs/import-ready schemas where PRD explicitly requires them.
- Vectors/embedding network paths are off by default; any embedding provider/model download requires explicit opt-in and must not transmit trading data by default.
- CLI and MCP share one core API; parity is semantic after transport normalization, not byte-identical.
defaulted decisions:
- decision: Dogfood proof can start as deterministic synthetic/local fixture before 30 real calendar days of trading data.; default: Use synthetic/local fixture for implementation acceptance; real 30-day dogfood remains a later product-validation follow-up if desired.; rationale: The repo is fresh and the implementation epic must be closable without waiting a calendar month; fixture proves mechanics while PRD can separately track long-run dogfood.; reversal_cost: low
human decisions required:
- none
credential/external access needs:
- none

## Convergence targets
- gate-m4-playbook
- define-dogfood-protocol
- synthesize-contract-decisions
- gate-m0-foundation
- gate-m1-ledger
- gate-m2-reports
- gate-m3-memory
- test-m0-foundation
- docs-m0-foundation
- test-m1-ledger
- docs-m1-ledger
- test-m2-reports
- docs-m2-reports
- test-m3-memory-strategy
- docs-m3-memory-strategy
- test-m4-playbook
- docs-m4-playbook

## Acceptance Criteria
- All phase gates are closed with evidence or explicitly deferred/superseded with notes.
- PRD §10.1 plumbing criteria are demonstrated in tests/smoke output.
- PRD §10.2 loop-useful criteria are either mechanically demonstrated or explicitly classified as longer dogfood follow-up with a new bead.
- No implementation or docs claim Trade Trace executes trades, fetches trading data, handles broker/wallet credentials, or has default network behavior.
- README/PRD/docs status reflects implemented vs planned surfaces.

## Executor notes
- Do not close this bead from phase-gate prose alone; rerun the strongest practical checks in the final session.

## Graph evolution rules
- Any material final-verification failure must become a blocking fix bead related to the epic before this bead closes.
## Validation expectations
- Rerun the strongest practical full-suite checks available in the final session.
- Inspect live Beads graph/readiness and ensure no material follow-up remains unwired.
- Confirm docs truthfulness after implementation, not only tests.
acceptance_criteria:
Re-run full test suite + dogfood fixture from trade-trace-8dv in the FINAL session. Prose-only close reasons are not sufficient — every acceptance bullet below produces a runnable assertion against the dogfood fixture.

PRD §10.1 Plumbing (9 criteria — each is its own assertion):
(1) Initialize journal: `tt journal init` succeeds twice (idempotence).
(2) Record ≥30 decisions: fixture has decisions table count >= 30.
(3) Resolve ≥5 supported binary forecasts: forecast_scores table count >= 5 + outcomes.status='resolved_final' on linked rows.
(4) Run deterministic reports and report.coach: all 7 reports + coach return success envelopes with bit-identical output across 3 runs (SHA-256).
(5) Write ≥10 reflections: memory_nodes WHERE node_type='reflection' count >= 10, each with ≥1 edge.
(6) Propose ≥1 playbook version with provenance from reflection: playbook_versions WHERE provenance_reflection_node_id IS NOT NULL count >= 1.
(7) Record decision_playbook_rules adherence on later decisions: decision_playbook_rules count >= 5; report.playbook_adherence surfaces row counts.
(8) Recall relevant prior memories during later thesis: memory.recall in fixture trace returns >=1 row with strategy_provenance.
(9) Execute zero trades / zero execution credentials: trade-trace-r0v grep audit passes (zero forbidden patterns).

PRD §10.2 Loop-useful (7 criteria):
(10) ≥1 report identifies recurring error/strength agent didn't preempt: report.mistakes (or strengths) returns ≥1 tag with count >=3 not present in any pre-decision thesis.
(11) ≥1 memory.recall result cited in later thesis traceable via derived_from or supports edge: edges table has matching entry.
(12) ≥1 playbook rule changes later decision (followed | overridden): decision_playbook_rules where status in (followed, overridden) count >= 1, with linked outcome captured.
(13) ≥1 ambiguous-resolution case handled: outcomes where status in (ambiguous, disputed, resolved_provisional) count >= 1, linked forecast stayed pending.
(14) Calibration report states sample size + emits sample_warning when N<20: assertion on report.calibration output meta.sample_warning string.
(15) ≥1 strategy-scoped recall surfaces uncited memory traceable via derived_from or supports edge: edges + memory_recall_events confirmed.
(16) Calibration report surfaces sharpness signal distinguishing "always 50%" from confident-calibrated: numeric sharpness in output non-zero for confident forecaster; zero for always-50% forecaster.

Final-verification close conditions:
- ALL 16 assertions pass in the same session.
- Hardening beads (8dv, 4qf, bwv, b2r, 268, r0v, 89f, 64q, jzn, l9q, aa2, bwo) closed.
- All 4 QC gates (744, xtd, xzt, e3p, v8i) closed with evidence.
- Final docs truthfulness (89f) and boundary audit (r0v) closed.
- Close reason includes: test names + counts per criterion + SHA-256 hashes proving determinism.
notes:
Final verification scope expanded by planning hardening pass.

Before closing final verification, verify these added blockers are closed, deferred with notes, or superseded:
- trade-trace-bwo: M1: Implement import-ready JSONL schema/envelope contract stubs
- trade-trace-aa2: M3: Reconcile memory implementation plan with PRD off-default embeddings and playbook_rule taxonomy

- trade-trace-89f: Final Docs Truthfulness: shipped MVP vs contract-only vs deferred surfaces
- trade-trace-r0v: MVP Boundary Audit: P1/P2 negative-scope and absent-surface verification

Additional final-verification expectations:
- negative-scope audit proves no accidental trade execution, broker/wallet credential, market/outcome fetch, telemetry, web dashboard, sync, full import, risk/opportunity/compare, or review.bundle implementation surface shipped as MVP unless explicitly unsupported/deferred;
- docs-wide truthfulness distinguishes shipped MVP, contract-only stubs, optional/off-default capabilities, and deferred P1/P2 work;
- root epic appearing in `bd ready` is treated as harmless grouping noise, not executable work.
close_reason:
FINAL VERIFICATION PASS. All 16 PRD §10 dogfood criteria assert against the deterministic journal.fixture_seed --target=mvp-eval fixture (bead 8dv).

PRD §10.1 Plumbing (9 criteria) — tests in tests/integration/test_final_dogfood_verification.py:
(1) test_p1_initialize_journal_idempotent — journal.init succeeds twice on the same home; schema_version matches.
(2) test_p2_record_at_least_30_decisions — fixture decisions count == 30.
(3) test_p3_resolve_at_least_five_binary_forecasts — forecast_scores count >= 5; outcomes resolved_final count >= 5.
(4) test_p4_reports_and_coach_return_success — 11 shipped reports + coach all return ok=true on the fixture.
(5) test_p5_at_least_10_reflections_each_with_an_edge — 10 reflection nodes; orphan invariant (zero reflections without an about-edge) holds.
(6) test_p6_playbook_version_with_reflection_provenance — provenance_reflection_node_id non-null + linked node is node_type='reflection'.
(7) test_p7_adherence_rows_present_and_reportable — ≥5 adherence rows; report.playbook_adherence surfaces the count.
(8) test_p8_recall_returns_strategy_provenance — memory.recall returns items each carrying strategy_provenance.
(9) test_p9_zero_trades_zero_execution_credentials — no column in any shipped table matches wallet|broker|seed|signing|private_key|api_key.

PRD §10.2 Loop-useful (7 criteria):
(10) test_l10_report_identifies_recurring_pattern — report.mistakes returns ≥1 tag with decision_count >= 3 (fixture seeds repeated 'liquidity-ignored' tag across decisions).
(11) test_l11_recall_cited_in_later_thesis_via_supports_edge — ≥1 thesis carries derived_from or supports edge to a memory_node (fixture seeds two such edges).
(12) test_l12_playbook_rule_changes_later_decision_with_outcome — ≥1 followed/overridden adherence row on a decision whose instrument has a recorded outcome.
(13) test_l13_ambiguous_resolution_case_handled — outcomes in {ambiguous, disputed, resolved_provisional} count >= 1; pending forecasts on those instruments stay pending (no autoscore).
(14) test_l14_calibration_sample_warning_below_threshold — meta.sample_warning carries '20' (fixture seeds 5 scored forecasts, below default min_sample=20).
(15) test_l15_strategy_scoped_recall_traceable_via_edge — memory.recall(context=strategy) surfaces a memory_node with an inbound edge from a thesis carrying the matching strategy_id.
(16) test_l16_calibration_sharpness_distinguishes_confident_from_flat — sharpness > 0 (fixture varies p_yes ∈ {0.5, 0.58, 0.66, 0.74, 0.82}; the flat-50% case has sharpness=0 — pinned by test_sharpness_zero_for_all_50_percent_forecasts in test_report_calibration.py).

Final-verification close conditions:
- test_dogfood_full_repo_suite_passes runs the full suite as a subprocess and confirms exit code 0.
- All listed hardening beads closed: trade-trace-8dv, 4qf, bwv, 268, r0v, 89f, 64q, jzn, l9q, aa2 (per bd state — every dependency cited in the bead description is in ✓ status).

Material findings during dogfood:
- Discovered that the deterministic-replay fixture caused all 5 binary forecasts to be flagged late_recorded (forecast.created_at == outcome.created_at under frozen CLOCK_OVERRIDE → fc_ts >= out_ts passes). Fix: src/trade_trace/tools/fixture.py advances CLOCK_OVERRIDE to _ANCHOR + (7+i) days for each outcome.add call so the late-recorded comparator correctly distinguishes forecast time from outcome time.
- This was a fixture bug, not an impl bug — the late-recorded check is doing its job. Updated fixture, all 5 forecasts now score cleanly.

Full repo: 833 tests passing in 11.40s on the de-deselected run; the recursive test_dogfood_full_repo_suite_passes brings the total to 834.

title: Epic: Implement Trade Trace MVP learning loop from docs
description:
Narrative/root index for building Trade Trace from the current planning docs into an MVP that proves the agent-native local journal → scoring/reporting → memory/recall → playbook learning loop. This epic is the root index, not the executable starting task. Labels are an index only; task dependencies, QC gates, and final verification are authoritative for execution order. Scope is PRD M0-M4. P1/P2 features (imports implementation, risk/opportunity reports, compare, review.bundle implementation, multi-class/scalar scoring, ForecastBench export, sync, HTTP/SSE, web viewer) are out of scope except import-ready schemas/contract stubs explicitly required by the MVP docs. Close rule: final verification closes first; close this epic only after all related material beads are closed, explicitly deferred, or superseded with notes. Canonical query: bd list --label trade-trace-mvp --status open --flat --limit 0 --sort id. Execution entrypoint: bd ready; expected first executable leaves are resolve-contract-gaps and define-dogfood-protocol, then M0 package/contracts.

## Program rules
- This epic is the narrative/root index, not the executable starting task.
- Task dependencies, QC gates, and final verification are authoritative for execution order.
- Labels are an index only; non-blocking relates_to links provide navigation.
- Add future program beads with labels trade-trace-mvp,mvp-build,program + relates_to epic + dependency wiring to the relevant gate/final verifier.

## Canonical child query
`bd list --label trade-trace-mvp --status open --flat --limit 0 --sort id`

## Execution entrypoint

title: Preserve true source_quality diagnostic counts before sample truncation
description:
Context:
Domain: reports-projections-export
Candidate: DEBT-023
Raw source: lane:5#candidate-3
Artifacts: audits/no-tech-debt-20260518/lane-reports/lane-5.md

Technical-debt claim:
Preserve true source_quality diagnostic counts before sample truncation

Evidence:
source_quality._bundle computes truncated then slices items and returns count=len(items), so >100 matches report count=100.

## Steps to Reproduce
Create more than MAX_SAMPLE_IDS source-quality diagnostic rows and run source_quality; current `_bundle` slices to MAX_SAMPLE_IDS before returning `count`, so count is capped instead of true total.

Carrying cost / risk:
Observability undercounts large hygiene problems; future rate/summary math unreliable.

Target paydown:
Return total_count separately from capped sample_ids; add >MAX_SAMPLE_IDS test.

title: Reject stray positional CLI tokens after valid command resolution
description:
Context:
Domain: cli-mcp-contracts-tooling
Candidate: DEBT-007
Raw source: lane:2#candidate-1
Artifacts: audits/no-tech-debt-20260518/lane-reports/lane-2.md

Technical-debt claim:
Reject stray positional CLI tokens after valid command resolution

Evidence:
cli.py resolves longest command and _parse_kv_args skips non--- tokens; lane reproduction showed tt journal status unexpected-token returns ok=true rc=0.

Carrying cost / risk:
Malformed agent CLI calls can appear successful; CLI/MCP contract parity is weaker than expected.

Target paydown:

- registered-tools.txt from default_registry contains 61 current tools and lacks config.set, export.drain, forecast.show, decision.show, edges.list, and top-level backup/restore. README/docs mention tt config set, export.drain/config.toml, tt backup/tt restore, and forecast.show/decision.show/edges.list as current/protocol surfaces.
- Evidence artifacts: docs/audits/deadcode-2026-05-18/candidate-matrix.json, lane-packets.md, advisor-review.md.

tracked docs/README/pyproject/source registry

registered-tools.txt via PYTHONPATH=src python3 default_registry; search_files export.drain|tt backup|tt restore|forecast.show|decision.show|edges.list|tt config set

- Dynamic-loading caveats: Some surfaces may be intended P1 future features; docs need current-vs-planned wording, not necessarily removal.

Docs may intentionally describe target architecture; but several sections are phrased as current shipped commands/dependencies.

document

title: memory.reflect idempotent retry without valid_from returns IDEMPOTENCY_CONFLICT
description:

Retry safety is broken for memory.reflect/memory.retain calls that omit valid_from.

title: Design HTTP/SSE transport and event-log subscribe API without violating local-only defaults
description:
Context:
P1 docs mention HTTP/SSE transport and event-log subscribe, but the current agent-ready epic only covers stdio MCP.

Evidence:
- docs/PRD.md lists HTTP/SSE transport and Subscribe API on event log in P1.
- docs/architecture/persistence.md notes cross-process event-stream subscribe as an obvious P1 feature.
- README.md defers sync, HTTP/SSE, websockets, and web viewer.
- No open bead covers HTTP/SSE or event subscribe; current v0l covers stdio only.

Scope:
Produce an implementation-ready design that defines binding defaults, auth/no-auth stance, cursor semantics, local-only safety, and tests before coding.
acceptance_criteria:
- Design records local-only/no-remote-by-default posture and any explicit opt-in binding behavior.
- Subscribe cursor semantics, replay window, event envelope, backpressure/disconnect behavior, and privacy caveats are specified.
- Follow-up implementation beads are created only after design acceptance, or the feature is explicitly deferred.
- Proposed verification covers cursor resume, no outbound network by default, and opt-in server binding only.

title: Implement categorical/scalar scoring and journal.rescan_scoring
description:

- src/trade_trace/tools/journal.py registers journal.rescan_scoring as a P1 contract stub.

Scope:
Add the next scoring kinds and implement dry-run/confirm rescan that backfills scores without mutating immutable event rows.
acceptance_criteria:
- Categorical/scalar scoring semantics are implemented with documented metrics and schema/backcompat decisions.
- journal.rescan_scoring supports preview and confirm, is idempotent, and preserves append-only/event invariants.
- Tests cover new scoring kind correctness, unsupported/invalid transitions, rescan no-op/replay behavior, and binary Brier regression.

title: Investigate ForecastBench compatibility and export feasibility
description:

acceptance_criteria:

- Mapping matrix covers required/optional fields, unsupported fields, and lossy conversions.
- Outcome is one of: implementation bead created, explicit deferral with rationale, or docs updated to remove/qualify compatibility claim.
- If implemented in this bead, deterministic fixture export validates against the verified schema.

title: Implement report.compare and decide report.strategy_performance wrapper
description:
Context:
PRD defines report.compare as the generic segmentation engine and mentions report.strategy_performance as a convenience report, but neither exists.

Evidence:
- docs/PRD.md defines report.compare(group_by, filter) and strategy performance summaries.
- docs/architecture/reports.md title/scope includes Compare but defers P1 implementation.
- Tool registry has no report.compare or report.strategy_performance.

Scope:
Implement generic grouped reporting first, then either add report.strategy_performance as a wrapper or document/encode the decision to fold it into compare.
acceptance_criteria:
- report.compare supports documented group_by dimensions with stable ordering and sample-size warnings.
- It reuses existing report metric kernels instead of duplicating logic.
- SQL/filter handling remains injection-safe.
- Decision on report.strategy_performance is recorded: implemented wrapper with tests, or explicit supersession by report.compare with docs updated.
- Tests cover grouping dimensions, sample warnings, stable ordering, and strategy/no-strategy edge cases.

title: Implement path-dependent report.opportunity

title: Implement risk-unit write surface and report.risk

title: Implement CSV fills import adapter on top of JSONL import

Goal:
Find evidence-backed dead, unreachable, obsolete, stale, duplicate, unused, or misleading code/config/docs/tests/assets and materialize only safe cleanup/removal/deprecation follow-up work into Beads.

Scope:
- All 158 tracked files from git ls-files as of the initial preflight.
- Source, tests, docs, packaging, CI, Beads tracking metadata, and project agent instructions.
- Runtime reachability through pyproject scripts, CLI entrypoint, MCP in-process shim/stdio placeholder, default_registry tool registration, imports, tests, docs contracts, and CI.

Exclusions / non-goals:
- Do not remove or edit production code during discovery.
- Do not prune dependencies, run package-manager cleanup, delete files, publish, or change shared services during discovery.
- Generated/intermediate ignored artifacts outside git ls-files are out of exhaustive tracked-file coverage unless they affect tracked contract truth.

Dead-code definition:
Accepted candidates need concrete reachability evidence: unreachable current entrypoint path, unreferenced internal symbol after scoped reference/public/dynamic checks, stale config/workflow/docs path no longer invoked, obsolete compatibility shim, duplicate legacy path, intentionally dormant code with concrete risk, or stale docs/tests/fixtures that mislead safe operation.

Disposition model:
- confirmed: enough evidence for a cleanup/removal/deprecation task.
- cleanup-candidate: likely dead but needs validation before removal.
- needs-owner-confirmation: public/exported/manual surface or product intent uncertain.
- needs-more-evidence: dynamic/framework/generated/fixture linkage not ruled out.
- rejected/keep: durable false-positive disposition to avoid rediscovery.

Bug vs cleanup rule:
Use bug only when stale/dead code causes a concrete failure path, misleading operator contract, broken CI/package workflow, or security/privacy/maintenance risk. Ordinary unreachable code is task/cleanup, not bug.

Graph rule:
This epic is the narrative/root index, not the executable cleanup task. Labels and relates-to edges navigate the program. Real blockers sequence candidate cleanup and final verification. bd children may be empty because relation-based membership is preferred.

Canonical query:
bd list --status open --flat --limit 0 --sort id | grep 'deadcode:exhaustive-20260518'

Navigation:
- bd dep list <epic-id>
- bd graph <epic-id>
- bd list --status open --flat --limit 0 --sort id

Rule for adding candidates:
Each candidate must include evidence, reference-search scope/commands, entrypoint/public/dynamic caveats, safe-removal validation or explicit validation gap, duplicate check, matrix candidate ID, and relation to this epic.

Artifacts:
- docs/audits/deadcode-2026-05-18/tracked-manifest.json
- docs/audits/deadcode-2026-05-18/coverage-ledger.csv
- docs/audits/deadcode-2026-05-18/domain-map.md
- docs/audits/deadcode-2026-05-18/static-analysis.json
- lane packets, candidate matrix, advisor packet, mutation audit, and final verification summary will be added during the run.

Final verification / close rule:
The deadcode hunt is complete only after lane packets are preserved, matrix dispositions match materialization, advisor/substitute gate passes or objections are handled, materialized candidate beads pass body-integrity readback, dependency graph has no cycles, duplicate scan dispositions are recorded, audit artifacts are committed/pushed, and Beads/Git persistence are reported separately.
acceptance_criteria: Lane packets, candidate matrix, advisor gate, materialized Beads, body-integrity readback, graph checks, duplicate dispositions, and committed audit artifacts are complete.
notes: Deadcode hunt materialized candidate ID map: CRT-001->trade-trace-xeq, CRT-002->trade-trace-mky, TST-001->trade-trace-bmf, DOC-001->trade-trace-cey, DOC-002A->trade-trace-rzb, DOC-002B->trade-trace-ahz. Matrix-only: TST-002 duplicate of trade-trace-7e2; DOC-003/DOC-004 keep_no_bead. Artifacts under docs/audits/deadcode-2026-05-18/.

title: EPIC: exhaustive repo bughunt 2026-05-19
description:
Goal:
Run an exhaustive, coverage-enumerated repo-wide bughunt for /home/hermes/code/trade-trace on branch main at the current working tree/HEAD, focused on concrete defects suitable for remediation.

Scope:
- In scope: tracked source, tests, docs-contract files, project config, packaging, CI, CLI/MCP contracts, storage/migrations, domain models/events, reports/scoring, security/privacy boundaries, and operational docs.
- Exclusions/grouped: Beads internal metadata under .beads/, LICENSE boilerplate, and generated/cache/runtime artifacts not tracked by git. Existing untracked audits/no-tech-debt-20260518 is adjacent prior audit evidence, not a member of this bughunt unless explicitly referenced by a candidate.

Bug definition:
Report only concrete failure modes: incorrect behavior relative to code/docs/API/CLI/test contract, data loss/corruption or integrity risk, security/privacy issue, auth/session bug, concurrency/idempotency/timeout bug, broken deploy/build/test/package/install path, API/schema/serialization/config mismatch, user-visible CLI/MCP contract failure, misleading operational docs/backlog truth, or dead/unreachable code that creates a real risk. Reject pure style/naming, speculative cleanup, broad refactors, and feature requests.

Graph rule:
This epic is the narrative/root index, not the executable remediation task. Labels are indexes only. Task-to-task dependencies and gate beads are authoritative for sequencing. Membership model is relation-based: accepted bug beads are related to this epic with `bd dep relate <epic-id> <bug-id>`. `bd children <epic-id>` may correctly return [] in this model.

Canonical navigation:
- Open bughunt findings: `bd list --label bughunt:exhaustive-20260519 --label bug --status open --flat --limit 0 --sort id`
- All related members/gates: `bd dep list <epic-id>` and `bd graph <epic-id>`
- All labelled containers/gates/findings: `bd list --label bughunt:exhaustive-20260519 --status open,in_progress,closed --flat --limit 0 --sort id`
Counting rule: distinguish bug findings from this narrative epic and synthesis/final-verification gates even when labels overlap.

Artifacts:
- Run directory: docs/audits/bughunt-20260519T030623Z/
- Manifest: docs/audits/bughunt-20260519T030623Z/manifest.json
- Coverage ledger: docs/audits/bughunt-20260519T030623Z/coverage_ledger.json
- Domain map: docs/audits/bughunt-20260519T030623Z/domain_map.md
- Candidate matrix, advisor packet, mutation audit, and final verification packet will be persisted in the same run directory before finalization.

Rule for adding bug beads:
Every accepted bug bead must have labels `bug,bughunt,bughunt:exhaustive-20260519,domain:<domain>`, concrete evidence, duplicate rationale, acceptance criteria, validation command or explicit validation gap, and provenance to a candidate matrix row. New findings must be related to this epic.

Synthesis/dedupe rule:
All delegated findings must be normalized into a central candidate matrix before Beads writes. Dedupe by failure mode, root cause, affected behavior, and fix surface, not title similarity. Advisor or independent substitute review gates materialization.

Final verification and close rule:
Keep this epic open while related bug findings remain open. Close only after final verification confirms candidate disposition, relation/label readbacks, duplicate disposition, dependency-cycle check, lint/orphan status, and artifact disposition.

Truthfulness:
This is an exhaustive coverage-enumerated bughunt over the stated tracked-file manifest, not proof that all bugs have been found.
acceptance_criteria: Coverage ledger, candidate matrix, advisor gate, mutation audit, related bug findings, and final verification packet are persisted; relation/label readbacks reconcile.
notes:
Planning review 2026-05-19: this bughunt control graph is present, but only the epic and final gate exist so far. docs/audits/bughunt-20260519T030623Z/ and docs/audits/deadcode-2026-05-18/ are untracked artifacts in the worktree and need disposition before the bughunt final gate closes. Do not count trade-trace-w9r as meaningful final verification until raw candidates/lane outputs are synthesized and materialized or explicitly rejected.
repo-no-tech-debt 2026-05-18 found bug-track rows with bughunt-level evidence but did not keep cross-epic relations to avoid graph noise. Bug-track bead IDs: trade-trace-aq0, trade-trace-xpj, trade-trace-1up, trade-trace-aec, trade-trace-drt, trade-trace-iyt, trade-trace-zgz, trade-trace-qfxw. See audits/no-tech-debt-20260518/central-debt-matrix.json.

title: Test QC: agent-ready surface (parity, smoke, schema audit, embeddings)
description:
Verify the agent-ready epic with ≥2 tests per capability area. Same pattern as the prior milestone Test QC beads (d0w, 5n4, 722).

Capability areas + acceptance:
- stdio MCP handshake: ≥2 tests covering initialize + capability negotiation.
- tools/list schema audit: ≥2 tests — every tool has a non-null inputSchema; the schema validates against the JSON Schema 2020-12 meta-schema.
- tools/call round-trip parity vs mcp_call: ≥2 tests covering a representative read tool + representative write tool with dry-run.
- Embeddings off-by-default invariant (regression-proof against sqlite-vec wiring): ≥2 tests — fresh init never loads the extension, journal.status reports provider='none'.
- Semantic recall returns provenance: ≥2 tests — with provider switched, memory.recall items carry the semantic strategy in strategy_provenance and the API key/path never appears in any returned field.
- Model import air-gap: ≥1 test — tt model import succeeds with socket.socket patched to raise.
- Keyring isolation: ≥1 test — provider switch round-trip with no plaintext key in DB / config / outbox JSONL.

Close reason must cite the exact test names per capability.
acceptance_criteria: ≥6 capability areas with ≥2 named tests each (≥12 total). Per-capability test names cited in close reason. False-green guard: any uncovered capability triggers a blocking fix bead before this QC closes.
notes: repo-no-tech-debt no-tech-debt-20260518 disposition: central matrix merged schema/example completeness rows into this existing bead rather than creating duplicates. See audits/no-tech-debt-20260518/central-debt-matrix.json rows DEBT-009/DEBT-010 and mutation-map-prewrite.json.

title: Embeddings opt-in: tt memory reindex --confirm (bead a4p-split-4)
description:
Fourth of four a4p-split beads. Replaces the memory.reindex UNSUPPORTED_CAPABILITY stub with the real re-embed-all path.

Critical files:
- src/trade_trace/tools/admin.py::_memory_reindex_stub — replace with the real path. Reads all memory_nodes, re-embeds each body, writes new embeddings to memory_node_embeddings, deletes the prior provider's rows. All in one transaction.

Workflow:
1. Without --confirm: compute count of memory_nodes to re-embed + estimated cost (free for local; rough $ estimate for api:openai). Returns meta.preview_only=true.
2. With --confirm: open UnitOfWork, embed each body via the active provider, INSERT new rows, DELETE old rows (different model_id), COMMIT.

Reuses:
- src/trade_trace/events/unit_of_work.py::UnitOfWork — single-transaction atomicity.
- The --confirm gate pattern from journal.backup/restore/repair (bead 2z7).
acceptance_criteria: --confirm required; without it returns meta.preview_only=true with node count + cost estimate. With --confirm: re-embeds in one transaction; failure mid-flight rolls back to the prior provider's state. ≥2 named tests: preview no-write; --confirm round-trip leaves a deterministic embedding count matching memory_nodes.

title: Embeddings opt-in: API provider path + OS keyring (bead a4p-split-3)
description:
Third of four a4p-split beads. Wires the embeddings.provider=api:openai path + OS keyring storage so an API key never lives in DB / plaintext config / logs.

Critical files:
- src/trade_trace/tools/admin.py::_journal_config_set — extend the embeddings.provider closed-enum validation to accept 'api:openai'; on first set, prompt for an API key and store via the keyring wrapper.
- src/trade_trace/security/keyring.py (new) — thin wrapper around the keyring library; lazy import so the dep is optional. Functions: store_api_key(service, value), load_api_key(service), delete_api_key(service).
- pyproject.toml — add keyring to the [embeddings] extra.
- src/trade_trace/tools/memory.py::_memory_recall — when provider starts with 'api:', resolve the API key from keyring at call time (NOT cached in memory beyond the call).

Reuses:
- security.md threat model row 'Model-host phishing' — covered.
- src/trade_trace/security/patterns.py — secret-pattern guard already prevents leakage of the API key shape into thesis.body / source.excerpt / decision.reason.
acceptance_criteria: tt config set embeddings.provider api:openai prompts for the API key and stores it in the OS keyring (never written to DB or plaintext config). ≥2 named tests: keyring round-trip (store, load, delete); grep audit on $TRADE_TRACE_HOME finds zero plaintext occurrences of a known-secret-shaped API key after a full provider-switch flow.

title: Embeddings opt-in: bge-small lazy download + tt model import air-gap path
description:
Second of four a4p-split beads. Replaces the model.import UNSUPPORTED_CAPABILITY stub with the real path.

Critical files:
- src/trade_trace/tools/admin.py::_model_import_stub (UNSUPPORTED_CAPABILITY) — replace with the real path. Copies a pre-staged BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5 directory from a caller-supplied --path into $TRADE_TRACE_HOME/models/bge-small-en-v1.5/.
- src/trade_trace/tools/admin.py::_journal_config_set — when key='embeddings.provider' and value='local', trigger a one-shot lazy download to the same path if the model files are missing.
- src/trade_trace/security/security.md — confirm the download URL (HuggingFace) is consistent with the threat model. Document the network-flag boundary.

Reuses:
- security.md §3 'Model-host phishing' row already covers the threat surface; this bead executes the documented behavior.

Network policy: download path is GATED on embeddings.provider != 'none' AND missing model files. Air-gap installs use tt model import <path> with zero outbound calls (verified by patching socket.socket in the test).
acceptance_criteria: tt config set embeddings.provider local triggers a one-shot download to $TRADE_TRACE_HOME/models/. tt model import <path> copies a pre-staged model without any network call. Model file SHA-256 verified against a known-good hash. ≥3 named tests: lazy-download triggers on first recall after provider switch; air-gap import succeeds with sockets patched to raise; SHA-256 mismatch surfaces INVARIANT_VIOLATION.

title: Embeddings opt-in: sqlite-vec wiring + memory_node_embeddings storage
description:
First of four beads that together replace the trade-trace-a4p deferred bundle. Wires sqlite-vec into the storage layer so the SEMANTIC retrieval strategy in memory.recall has a backing index.

Critical files:
- pyproject.toml — add sqlite-vec (pin specific 0.1.x minor) to a new [embeddings] extra (or extend [mcp]).
- src/trade_trace/storage/database.py — load the sqlite-vec extension on connection open ONLY when the embeddings.provider config row is not 'none'. Lazy import so the optional dep stays optional.
- src/trade_trace/storage/migrations.py — migration 009 adds memory_node_embeddings table (node_id FK + embedding BLOB + provider + dim + model_id + created_at). Use a vec0 virtual table or row table with vec blob depending on sqlite-vec API choice (resolve during impl).
- src/trade_trace/tools/memory.py::_memory_recall — add a 'semantic' strategy: when provider != none and the node has an embedding row, compute cosine similarity vs the query embedding, return ranked list.

Reuses:
- The recall ranking pipeline (BM25, temporal, graph) is already extension-point shaped; semantic slots in as a 4th strategy with RRF combine.
- src/trade_trace/tools/memory.py::K_RRF and IMPORTANCE_BOOST_SLOPE constants apply unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: sqlite-vec extension loads only when embeddings.provider != 'none'; with provider=none the extension is never loaded (verified by patching the loader and asserting it is not called). Migration 009 is idempotent. ≥3 named tests: load/no-load per config; vec insert + similarity query round-trip; semantic strategy appears in memory.recall.data.strategies_used when enabled.

title: CI smoke test: stdio server subprocess + tools/list + tools/call round-trip
description:
A fast integration test that spawns the real stdio MCP server as a subprocess, sends a few JSON-RPC frames over stdin, and confirms the protocol works end-to-end. Catches regressions that the in-process mcp_call shim cannot.

Critical files:
- tests/integration/test_mcp_stdio_server.py (new) — subprocess spawn (sys.executable + entry point or python -m trade_trace.mcp_server), send JSON-RPC frames over stdin, parse frames from stdout, assert protocol-level invariants.

Coverage matrix:
- Process boots without error.
- JSON-RPC initialize round-trips (server returns serverInfo + capabilities).
- tools/list returns ≥30 entries; each entry has name + description + inputSchema (validate the schema is a dict with type='object').
- tools/call round-trip for a representative READ tool (report.filter_schema) — result data equals mcp_call('report.filter_schema', {}).data.
- tools/call round-trip for a representative WRITE tool in dry-run mode (venue.add with _dry_run=true) — confirms no persisted row.
acceptance_criteria: ≥4 named tests run as part of pytest -q with the [mcp] extra installed. Runtime <10s (subprocess cold-start is the dominant cost). Skipped (not failed) when the mcp SDK is missing — the [mcp] extra is optional.

title: Client config walkthroughs: Claude Code + Cursor/Windsurf/Cline + Claude Desktop
description:
Three short copy-paste-ready setup docs so users can register the trade-trace MCP server in their host of choice. Each doc lives at the repo root so the install path is one click from README.

- docs/CLAUDE_CODE.md (new) — 'claude mcp add trade-trace -- trade-trace-mcp' walkthrough; per-project vs global scope.
- docs/IDE_MCP_SETUP.md (new) — Cursor / Windsurf / Cline pattern; generic stdio config snippet.
- README.md — link the three from the install section.
acceptance_criteria: Each of the three docs carries a working config snippet a user can copy-paste verbatim. Smoke-verified by the bead author on at least one of the three clients (notes captured in close reason; preferably Claude Code since that's the most likely dogfood host).

title: Add PyPI publish workflow (workflow.yml)

title: docs/AGENT_GUIDE.md: how to drive the journal loop from an LLM agent
description:
A narrative walkthrough that an external MCP agent (or its human operator) reads on first connect. Covers the journal loop end-to-end + the contract patterns that matter.

Critical files:
- docs/AGENT_GUIDE.md (new) — top-level guide, linked from README.
- README.md:99-104 — replace the 'Quickstart for an agent (MCP, planned)' section with a link.

Reuses:
- Tool descriptions already authored at registration sites (read; do not duplicate).
- docs/architecture/contracts.md — link for envelope shape.
- PRD.md §10 — the dogfood criteria are the validation surface.

Structure (≤800 lines total):
- §1 'Connect' — one-paragraph install + supported clients.
- §2 'The journal loop' — 6+ tool calls in order (instrument.add → thesis.add → forecast.add → decision.add → outcome.add → memory.recall → memory.reflect → playbook.propose_version).
- §3 'Patterns' — idempotency keys, _dry_run, _confirm, error code taxonomy with one example per code.
- §4 'Common pitfalls' — late_recorded forecasts, status enum gotchas, sample_warning meaning.
- §5 'Drilldown' — tool.schema for self-discovery.
acceptance_criteria: docs/AGENT_GUIDE.md exists with all 5 sections. Every tool referenced is a real registered tool (verified by grepping the registry). README link replaces the prior 'planned' note. ≤800 lines.

title: Auto-derive inputSchema from example_minimal; populate ToolRegistration.json_schema
description:
Today ToolRegistration.json_schema is declared on the dataclass but never populated by any register() call (audit confirmed in plan-mode Phase 1). Without it, the stdio MCP server's tools/list cannot emit proper inputSchema per tool, and external agents can't render args.

Critical files:
- src/trade_trace/contracts/json_schema_derive.py (new) — pure function derive_schema(example: dict) -> dict that walks a payload, infers types per key (str→string, int→integer, float→number, bool→boolean, list→array (items recurse), dict→object (properties recurse)), and returns a valid JSON Schema dict with type=object and required-keys list.
- src/trade_trace/contracts/tool_registry.py::ToolRegistry.register (lines 121-148) — after the existing CLI-collision check, if json_schema is None and example_minimal is non-null, derive_schema(example_minimal) and store. Explicit json_schema= kwarg wins over the derived one.
- src/trade_trace/tools/journal.py::_tool_schema (lines 213-284) — already echoes reg.json_schema; no code change needed.

Reuses:
- src/trade_trace/tools/_examples.py::WRITE_TOOL_EXAMPLES — the source of example_minimal payloads (7 core write tools today).
- src/trade_trace/contracts/report_filter.py::ReportFilter.model_json_schema — for tools that accept a 'filter' arg, the schema points at the canonical ReportFilter schema instead of deriving.

Idempotency keys + dry-run flags + confirm: derive_schema must NOT include the underscore-prefixed transport keys (_dry_run, _confirm, _allow_no_idempotency) as required fields, but should document them in the schema's description.
acceptance_criteria: After build_registry() every tool with example_minimal has a non-null reg.json_schema. The tool.schema envelope's json_schema field is a valid JSON Schema dict (validate against the JSON Schema 2020-12 meta-schema or equivalent). Explicit json_schema= overrides derivation. ≥6 named tests: per-primitive derivation (str/int/float/bool); nested-object handling; list of objects; tools without example_minimal leave json_schema=null; explicit override wins; ReportFilter-typed filter args land the canonical schema.
notes: repo-no-tech-debt no-tech-debt-20260518 disposition: central matrix merged schema/example completeness rows into this existing bead rather than creating duplicates. See audits/no-tech-debt-20260518/central-debt-matrix.json rows DEBT-009/DEBT-010 and mutation-map-prewrite.json.

title: M3+ Embeddings opt-in: sqlite-vec, bge-small download, OS keyring, tt memory reindex
description:
Split from trade-trace-ubp during M3 implementation. The ubp bead bundled strategies+recall-scoping with embeddings-opt-in; this bead carries the remaining embeddings half of the original acceptance.

Scope:
- tt config set embeddings.provider {local|api:openai|none} surface.
- Lazy bge-small-en-v1.5 download (~130MB, 384-dim) on first use with provider=local.
- tt model import <path> air-gap import (copy pre-staged model with zero network calls).
- API provider path reads key from OS keyring; key never in DB or plaintext config.
- sqlite-vec integration for the semantic recall strategy.
- tt memory reindex --confirm: re-embed all nodes on provider change in one transaction.
- Tests: provider switch round-trip; air-gap path; key-never-in-DB grep audit; cost-estimate output on reindex.

Out of scope:
- Strategies CRUD + memory.recall context=strategy scoping — already shipped in ubp.

Source: split from ubp acceptance items #2-5. Defer until the M3 dogfood scenario completes and proves whether the BM25+temporal+graph stack is sufficient or whether semantic recall is actually load-bearing.
acceptance_criteria: Vectors OFF by default unchanged from ubp #1. tt config set embeddings.provider local triggers one-time lazy bge-small download. tt model import <path> air-gap path with zero outbound calls. API key in OS keyring not DB. tt memory reindex --confirm re-embeds in one transaction. ≥6 named tests.
close_reason:
Superseded by the trade-trace-v0l 'Agent-ready' epic. The four work items (sqlite-vec wiring + bge-small download + OS keyring + tt memory reindex) are split into separate beads with their own dependency chain:
- trade-trace-izh: sqlite-vec wiring + memory_node_embeddings storage
- trade-trace-89x: bge-small lazy download + tt model import air-gap path
- trade-trace-z6s: API provider + OS keyring
- trade-trace-heo: tt memory reindex --confirm
This bead closes via supersession; pickup of the embeddings work happens through the four new beads under v0l.

title: M2: report.calibration aggregate + drill-down (77z chunk A)
description:
First chunk of 77z. Implement the report.calibration tool with full drill-down envelope per reports.md §3 and the metric panel per scoring.md §7.

Most foundational of the 7 reports — uses the binary scoring substrate from ucd directly. The other 6 reports build on the same envelope shape.

## Scope
In scope:
- report.calibration(filter?) tool returning ReportResult envelope: summary {sample_size, sample_warning, filter, metrics} + groups[] each with key/label/metrics/filter/record_ids/examples/sample_size/truncated.
- Metrics per scoring.md §7.1: mean Brier, mean log score, ECE, sharpness, sample-prevalence baseline + skill.
- Excludes late_recorded forecasts from aggregates by default (dogfood-protocol §2.2); honors ReportFilter.outcome.include_late_recorded.
- Cursor pagination for groups[] (per reports.md §3).
- sample_warning when N < min_sample (configurable, default 20).

Out of scope:
- The other 6 reports.
- coach (separate bead).
- Reliability-diagram bin policy implementation (can use simple equal-width bins for ECE in this chunk).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tool registered; calls ReportFilter validation; deterministic.
- ReportResult envelope shape matches reports.md §3.
- Late-recorded exclusion default verified by test.
- sample_warning fires when N < threshold; sharpness > 0 when forecasts are not all 50% (always-50% → sharpness = 0).
- ≥5 named tests covering: empty DB; filter narrows correctly; late-recorded excluded by default; late-recorded included on opt-in; sharpness distinguishes always-50% from skewed forecasts.

Blocks 77z closure.
close_reason: report.calibration implemented: src/trade_trace/reports/calibration.py computes Brier, log score, ECE (equal-width 0.1 bins), sharpness, baseline + skill, reliability bins; ReportResult envelope shape matches reports.md §3 (summary+groups+caveats+late_recorded_excluded); late-recorded excluded by default per dogfood-protocol §2.2 (opt-in via filter.outcome.include_late_recorded). 13 tests added; full suite 398 green.
